summary: Typosquat of @solana-labs/anchor exfiltrates host data and executes shell commands at install
details: Package name appears to impersonate the legitimate @solana-labs/anchor (Solana smart-contract framework). package.json declares "postinstall": "node install.js", which auto-executes on `npm install`. install.js imports `os`, `fs`, `https`, and `child_process`; collects host identifiers (`os.hostname()`, `os.userInfo()`, `process.platform`); checks for sensitive paths via `fs.existsSync(...)`; issues HTTPS POST requests; and invokes `execSync('curl...')` and `execSync('powershell...')` for OS-specific command execution. The combination of automatic install-time execution, system-information collection, outbound HTTPS POST, and shell-command execution via curl/PowerShell on a name-squatted package is the canonical install-time credential/host exfiltration + dropper pattern.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
